epigraph.quote: Dylan played his recorder as the sun set & it was beautiful, beautiful.

moments.quote: We wanted to see a restored Martello Tower No 24 at near Hythe, Dymchurch, but when we got there, it was closed
We caught an earlier [?train] + in under 1½ hours we reached the campsite
Very green, a grassy valley, cold showers, fresh bread deliveries for breakfast.
So we drove to Abbeville. After a 2 hour wait, the garage reopened ... the Abbeville garage used one that someone else had already ordered & we were mended.
a drive-in campsite in a village, pretty beech hedges, choose your own plot
the hottest I've every been in my life - sitting streaming with sweat. In the middle we had supper on the grass - delicious paniers arrived. Sweat poured down. Fabulous, fabulous duets.
It's a very eccentric campsite in scorched orchards.
We set off & shopped outside St. Flour.
It was as a result of building the viaduct that Eiffel gained in confidence to build the Eiffel Tower. It's very impressive.
We had a mega meal at the farmhouse which started at 7.30 & finished after 10.
After supper, Simon + I walked up a lane to look at it in the sunset, a marvellous site, contrasting with the stink of an enormous pile of chickensh*t on the hillside.
The dog poo in Millan is appalling. Simon trod in a really soupy one.
one of the best campsites we've ever been in

images.caption: Musée de Millan painted + embroidered gloves made in Millan

moments.quote: The Abbey is as lovely as ever.
Girls were playing with lizards in a basin.
an American has made a large copy of the Rosetta Stone in Zimbabwean granite.
a lovely campsite in an enormous field with walnut trees

images.caption: l'Oasienne camping chambres d'hôtes near Figeac

moments.quote: the current was pretty strong in parts & we bumped our bums
they tricked the Saracens by presenting them with a fat calf & a bale of hay, & the Saracens gave up.

images.caption: Uzerche, church of St. Peter + other views of the walled town

moments.quote: We walked through beautiful woods & went to supper at Margaret's hotel.
Simon, Eve, Charles, Margaret & Helen left after a leisurely breakfast. We look at the view from Mont Gargan. We drove north
we had already put up the tent at Courcheveigny municipal camping ... near Chambord ... We went back to Courcheveigny for supper.
We picked up M+H & drove past Chateau de Chambord.
Just south of Amiens, St. Fuscien, is Margaret's hotel. We dropped off her case ... we found a very good place to eat supper, overlooking the boat-gardens, Hortillages.
Simon drove into Amiens & we found a very good place to eat supper, overlooking the boat-gardens, Hortillages. Lovely scenery.

images.caption: weeping on the tomb of Guillan Lucas, Amiens Cathedral

moments.quote: we could hurry through to get on the train (the numbering system for calling us was unclear) but then we just sat infront of the barrier + missed the train.
we showed her grandson, little Harry, the camping van
